title: RUQ Pain After Meals - Acute Cholecystitis
chief_complaint: Severe right upper quadrant pain for 6 hours after eating fatty meal. Nausea and vomiting. Known gallstones on previous ultrasound.

physical_exam.abdomen: RUQ tenderness, Murphy sign positive, voluntary guarding

hidden_data.us_abd: Gallstones, gallbladder wall thickened 5mm, pericholecystic fluid, sonographic Murphy sign positive

key_learning_points: Murphy sign = inspiratory arrest on RUQ palpation
NPO, IV fluids, antibiotics, pain control

patient_responses.pain_trigger: I ate fried chicken and fries, then the pain started
patient_responses.similar: I've had milder pain before after heavy meals

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Acute cholecystitis - Murphy sign is classic.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is acute cholecystitis. The Murphy sign + ultrasound findings confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Acute cholecystitis: NPO, IV fluids, antibiotics (ceftriaxone + metronidazole), early laparoscopic cholecystectomy within 72h. If high surgical risk, consider percutaneous cholecystostomy.
